title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Feast
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. Discover rich Middle Eastern flavors and create a show-stopping dish for your next dinner.

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Lamb Ouzi whole slow roasted, served with spiced rice, nuts, and fresh herbs, a traditional Middle Eastern dish.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of a traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection, a true feast for any occasion.

description: Experience the grandeur of Lamb Ouzi, a magnificent Middle Eastern dish featuring tender, slow-roasted lamb infused with aromatic spices. This celebratory meal combines succulent meat with fragrant rice, creating a truly unforgettable feast. Perfect for gatherings, it promises a symphony of rich flavors in every bite.

ingredients.notes: mixture of cumin, coriander, cardamom, cinnamon, allspice, black pepper

instructions.title: Prepare and Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Season the lamb pieces generously with half of the ouzi spice blend, salt, and black pepper. Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb pieces in batches until browned on all sides, then remove them from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In the same pot, add another tablespoon of ghee or olive oil. Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it softens and becomes translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Lamb
instructions.text: Return the seared lamb pieces to the pot. Stir in the remaining ouzi spice blend. Pour in 2 cups of the hot chicken or lamb broth. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender enough to be easily shredded.

instructions.title: Cook the Spiced Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb simmers, drain the soaked basmati rice. In a separate medium pot, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of ghee or olive oil. Add the drained rice and stir for 1-2 minutes to lightly toast it. Pour in the remaining 1 cup of hot broth, season with a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes until all liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Assemble the Ouzi
instructions.text: Once the lamb is tender, you can shred it slightly with two forks if you prefer, or leave it in delicious chunks. Gently fold the cooked lamb and any remaining flavorful cooking liquid into the fluffy basmati rice. Mix carefully to combine all ingredients without mashing the rice.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the Lamb Ouzi to a large, beautiful serving platter. Garnish generously with the toasted pine nuts, toasted slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately as a complete and flavorful Middle Eastern feast, perhaps alongside a fresh cucumber yogurt salad.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, ensure your ouzi spice blend is freshly ground. This makes a significant difference in the aroma and taste of the dish.
Do not overcook the rice; it should be fluffy and separate, not mushy. Letting it rest covered after cooking helps it steam evenly and retain its texture.
For an even richer flavor, you can add a tablespoon of tomato paste to the lamb simmering step, or a few strands of saffron infused in a little hot water to the rice.
Lamb Ouzi is excellent for meal prep. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
article: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a beloved Middle Eastern Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
